day1:
Day 1 — Qualifying Arena
1. General Information
Date: June 13, 2026
Start Time: 12:00 UTC
Players: Open to everyone
Platform: https://anichess.com
Prize Pool: 20,000 CHECK tokens
Format: 3-hour Qualification Arena
Time Control: 5+2
Advancing: Top 4 players advance to Day 2 as Wild Cards

2. Scoring System
Win: 3 points
Draw: 1 points
Loss: 0 points
Tiebreak: Player with a higher  win rate

3. Arena Qualification Bonuses (20,000 CHECK)
5th–10th: 1,000 CHECK each | 11th–20th: 500 CHECK each | 21st–30th: 400 CHECK each | 31st–40th: 300 CHECK each | 41st–50th: 200 CHECK each

4. Competitive Integrity
Players may not:

- Receive outside assistance.
- Ghost stream opponents.
- Account share.
- Exploit bugs intentionally.

Penalties include:

- Game Loss
- Disqualification

5. Tournament Administration

Tournament officials have final authority on:

- Rules interpretation
- Technical disputes
- Scheduling issues
- Penalties
- Exceptional circumstances

Their decisions are final and binding.
day2:
Day 2 — Knockout Bracket
1. General Information
Date: June 14, 2026
Start Time: 12:00 UTC
Players: 16 (4 Wild Cards + 12 Elite Entrants)
Platform: https://anichess.com
Prize Pool: 80,000 CHECK tokens
Format: Single Elimination Bracket
Time Control: 5 minutes + 2 seconds increment
Advancing: Top 4 advance to the Grand Finals as Magnus Challenger
Mandatory Presence: All players must be present in https://discord.gg/anichess 1 hour before the start of the tournament for camera check.

2. Participants (total 16)
4 Wild Cards:
4 Top players from Day 1 Qualifying Arena

12 Elite Entrants:
2 highest rating player in Standard Mode: (as of  Jun 13 · 00:00 UTC) 
2 Former Anichess Champions (Freysa & Ellaijio)
5 Content Creators (invite-only)
1 Winner from Chess.com Tournament
1 Winner from Lichess Tournament
1 Winner from King's Gambit Leaderboard

3. Match Rules
Rounds: Best of 3
Colors: Higher seed plays White in Game 1. Colors alternate each game thereafter
Draw Policy: Game is voided and replayed with same colors. Spell reuse is allowed for the replay
Waiting Time: 5-minute grace period — no-show results in a technical loss
Anti-Cheat Policy: All players must turn on their camera during the match, failure to do so will result in penalty or disqualification.
IMPORTANT!!!: Spell Strategy Rule — all spells used within a match must be unique. Repeating a spell results in an immediate TECHNICAL LOSS for that game

4. Prize Distribution (80,000 CHECK)
1st–4th Place: 10,000 CHECK each | 5th–8th Place: 5,000 CHECK each | 9th–16th Place: 2,500 CHECK each

5. Competitive Integrity
Players may not:

- Receive outside assistance.
- Ghost stream opponents.
- Account share.
- Exploit bugs intentionally.

Penalties include:

- Warning
- Game Loss
- Match Loss
- Disqualification

6. Tournament Administration

Tournament officials have final authority on:

- Rules interpretation
- Technical disputes
- Scheduling issues
- Penalties
- Exceptional circumstances

Their decisions are final and binding.
finals:
Road to Magnus Finals
1. General Information
Date: June 16, 2026
Start Time: 9:30 UTC
Players: Magnus Carlsen +GM Oleksandr Bortnyk + the 4 Magnus Challengers
Platform: https://anichess.com
Prize Pool: 1,000,000 CHECK tokens
Format: Single Round Robin — each player competes in 5 games
Time Control: 3 minutes + 2 seconds increment
Pairings: Set by organizers in advance
Grand Final: Top 2 finishers from the Round Robin play a final match (BO1) to crown the champion
Mandatory Presence: All players must be present in https://discord.gg/anichess 30 minutes before the start of the tournament

2. Scoring System
Win: 1 point
Draw: 0.5 points
Loss: 0 points
Tiebreak: Berger coefficient — sum of points from defeated opponents plus half the points from drawn opponents

3. Match Rules
Rounds: Best of 1
Disconnects: Players are responsible for their own connection. No replays for disconnects
Disputes: All disputes are resolved exclusively by Anichess referees. All decisions are final
Waiting Time: 5-minute grace period — no-show results in a technical loss
Anti-Cheat Policy: All players must turn on their camera during the match, failure to do so will result in penalty or disqualification.

4. Prize Distribution (1,000,000 CHECK)
1st Place: 300,000 CHECK | 2nd Place: 250,000 CHECK | 3rd Place: 200,000 CHECK
4th Place: 125,000 CHECK | 5th Place: 75,000 CHECK | 6th Place: 50,000 CHECK

5. Competitive Integrity
Players may not:

- Receive outside assistance.
- Ghost stream opponents.
- Account share.
- Exploit bugs intentionally.

Penalties include:

- Warning
- Game Loss
- Disqualification

6. Tournament Administration

Tournament officials have final authority on:

- Rules interpretation
- Technical disputes
- Scheduling issues
- Penalties
- Exceptional circumstances

Their decisions are final and binding.
